header.headline: What the Honorary Consulate Provides
header.description: Five services. Monday appointments. Derry, NH. No drive to New York required for most consular needs.

notary-public.name: Notary Public
notary-public.tagline: For American documents requiring a notarial seal
notary-public.description: Wills, trusts, deeds, contracts, affidavits, and any American document requiring notarization under New Hampshire law.
whatToBring: The completed document (do not sign in advance)
Cash or check for $10 per notarial act

signature-authentication.name: Signature Authentication
signature-authentication.description: Official consular authentication of signatures on documents destined for Hungary. Required for property transactions, legal affairs, and official submissions to Hungarian authorities.
whatToBring: The completed document, ready to sign
Cash or check for $36 per document

copy-authentication.name: Copy Authentication
copy-authentication.tagline: Certified true copies for official use
copy-authentication.description: Official consular certification that a copy is a true and accurate reproduction of an original document. Required when originals cannot be submitted.
whatToBring: A clean, legible copy of the document
Cash or check for $24 per page

official-copy.name: Official Copy
official-copy.description: An officially presented copy issued directly by the consulate. Used when a document must be submitted as a consular exhibit.
whatToBring: Cash or check for $18 per page

life-certificate.name: Life Certificate
life-certificate.description: Required annually by Hungarian pension authorities to confirm that the recipient is living. Signed by the Honorary Consul.
whatToBring: Hungarian pension documentation showing your recipient number

scopeCallout.eyebrow: Not Sure if We Can Help?
scopeCallout.headline: See the full scope of services before you book.
scopeCallout.cta: What We Handle vs. New York →

disclaimer: Honorary consuls do not work in a formal office, nor do they have fixed working hours or schedules. They do not receive compensation from Hungary for their duties and therefore cannot be held accountable for their work schedules.
